title: Implicit authentication with sensor normalization and multi-modal domain adaption based on mobile crowd sensing
venue: CCF Transactions on Pervasive Computing and Interaction

method.input_representation: 10 items: time, acceleration (x, y, z), angular velocity (x, y, z), and Eulerian angles (pitch, roll, yaw)
method.training_strategy: Domain adaptation to transfer models from a source domain (abundant training data) to a target domain (small samples from actual scenarios).

evaluation.context: Achieved on multi-modal situations with different pattern passwords

evaluation_protocol: Comparison of OCSVM against iForest and LOF; evaluation of normalization and domain adaptation across heterogeneous devices (smartphones vs tablets).

reported_limitations: Training needs to be transferred to mobile devices for better feasibility

key_contribution: Proposed sensor normalization (S-T and S-S) to enable authentication across heterogeneous devices
Used domain adaptation to handle multi-modal/unpredictable user states and small sample sizes
main_strength: Effectively handles the transition between smartphones and tablets
main_weakness: Lack of active spoofing/presentation attack tests
insights.notes_for_review: The paper focuses on the transition between different device forms (phone to tablet) and the ability to authenticate with very few samples via transfer learning.
